channel.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive
channel.description: A daily analysis of all of the AI and global news of the day I find from subscribing to a bunch of interesting newsletters
channel.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
channel.itunes:summary: A daily analysis of all of the AI and global news of the day I find from subscribing to a bunch of interesting newsletters

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 19, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic CFO Krishna Rao's power behind the scenes securing $60B in funding and an $800B+ valuation path; SemiAnalysis founder Dylan Patel's rise as Silicon Valley's most influential AI analyst with $100M revenue; Jensen Huang's "electron to token" worldview and Nvidia's orchestration of the AI supply chain; AI employees declining tender offers at Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX as IPOs approach; and the math behind why investing in 150 companies beats a 30-company concentrated fund by 2x.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic CFO Krishna Rao's power behind the scenes securing $60B in funding and an $800B+ valuation path; SemiAnalysis founder Dylan Patel's rise as Silicon Valley's most influential AI analyst with $100M revenue; Jensen Huang's "electron to token" worldview and Nvidia's orchestration of the AI supply chain; AI employees declining tender offers at Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX as IPOs approach; and the math behind why investing in 150 companies beats a 30-company concentrated fund by 2x.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 18, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Dylan Patel and SemiAnalysis grabbing sway in Silicon Valley with exclusive Nvidia praise from Jensen Huang; China's DeepSeek raising outside capital for the first time at a $10B+ valuation; and Flock Safety's $8.4 billion valuation amid protests over police surveillance data sharing with immigration officials.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Dylan Patel and SemiAnalysis grabbing sway in Silicon Valley with exclusive Nvidia praise from Jensen Huang; China's DeepSeek raising outside capital for the first time at a $10B+ valuation; and Flock Safety's $8.4 billion valuation amid protests over police surveillance data sharing with immigration officials.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 17, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: The Information (OpenAI/Cerebras deal, enterprise AI competition), Substack (Project Mythos and JPMorgan financial risk analysis)
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: The Information (OpenAI/Cerebras deal, enterprise AI competition), Substack (Project Mythos and JPMorgan financial risk analysis)

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 16, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: The Information on Apple sending Siri staffers to coding boot camp and AI buying bots reshaping payments; DeepLearning.AI's The Batch on the latest AI research and industry developments; PitchBook's VC and startup funding news; and the AI Agenda newsletter covering the latest in artificial intelligence business and policy.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: The Information on Apple Siri coding boot camp and AI buying bots; DeepLearning.AI The Batch; PitchBook VC/startup news; and the AI Agenda newsletter.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 15, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Azeem Azhar's Exponential View on how armed security guards physically carried OpenAI o3 model weights in locked briefcases to Los Alamos' classified air-gapped Venado supercomputer — revealing how the US is quietly taking control of who gets access to frontier AI; Anthropic's Mythos model and its classified deployment cementing a new era of state-controlled AI diffusion; Spanish PM Pedro Sánchez's masterful Tsinghua University speech using Mateo Ricci's map to pitch EU-China partnership as the world's largest trading relationship amid US-China tension; the Sinica Podcast's Johns Hopkins SAIS conference on what the United States actually wants from China policy, featuring perspectives from former officials; and Brandon Brown's constitutional case for invoking the 25th Amendment as a check on presidential fitness.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Exponential View on armed briefcases carrying OpenAI o3 weights to Los Alamos classified networks and how the US controls frontier AI access; Anthropic's Mythos in classified deployment; Spanish PM Sánchez's Tsinghua speech using Mateo Ricci to pitch EU-China partnership; Sinica Podcast's SAIS conference on what the US wants from China; and a constitutional case for the 25th Amendment.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 14, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: SpaceX financials show Starlink now drives 61% of revenue and 63% EBITDA margin while rockets burn cash; data labelling startups Handshake and Mercor surge on AI training demand; solid-state battery hype debunked as Chinese and Western carmakers make exaggerated claims; Microsoft's data center strategy unravels — how Amy Hood's spending cuts let Google and Oracle grab key power sites; Fleet's 'Reinforcement Learning Gym' startup reaches $750M valuation with revenues jumping from $1M to $60M annualized in months; OpenAI Stargate executives defect to Meta's new compute unit led by Alexandr Wang; CoreWeave strikes multi-year deal with Anthropic as Sam Altman addresses Molotov cocktail attack on his home; Tesla wins EU driver assistance approval in Netherlands; Hong Kong licenses HSBC and Standard Chartered to issue stablecoins; and Exponential View on how the Middle East conflict is unexpectedly accelerating sustainable aviation fuel adoption.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: SpaceX Starlink dominates revenue (61%) and EBITDA (63%); Handshake and Mercor surge on AI training demand; solid-state battery hype debunked; Microsoft's data center strategy stumbles as Google and Oracle grab power sites; Fleet's RL Gym startup hits $750M valuation; OpenAI Stargate execs defect to Meta's compute unit; CoreWeave-Anthropic deal; Tesla EU driving software approval; Hong Kong stablecoin licenses; and Exponential View on war-accelerated sustainable aviation fuel adoption.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 13, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Why advanced AI reasoning is locked away — Anthropic gates Claude Mythos for cybersecurity use only as frontier capability moves to coalition-mediated access; Atlassian and Figma lead the enterprise software R&D arms race spending 51% and 97% of revenue on R&D vs. competitors' 19%; Meta's Claudeonomics tokenmaxxing leaderboard rises and falls as employees burn 73 trillion tokens in 30 days; new research vindicates neurosymbolic AI over pure LLMs using NeuroLogic Decoding; and Azeem Azhar on how Claude Mythos permanently fuses AI capability with danger, forcing a repricing of critical infrastructure cyber risk.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup: Anthropic gates Claude Mythos for cybersecurity; Atlassian and Figma lead enterprise R&D spending at 51% and 97% of revenue; Meta's Claudeonomics tokenmaxxing leaderboard drama; neurosymbolic AI vindicated over pure LLMs; and Azeem Azhar on how Claude Mythos permanently fuses AI capability with danger and forces repricing of critical infrastructure risk.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 12, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: AI hype hits the physical energy wall as hyperscaler capex outpaces grid capacity; The Information and Substack newsletters dive into systemic shocks, hardcoded AI logic, and what happens when infrastructure can't keep up with compute demand.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup: AI hype hits the physical energy wall as hyperscaler capex outpaces grid capacity; systemic shocks, hardcoded AI logic, and the limits of compute infrastructure.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 11, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Cisco in talks to acquire Israeli AI security startup Astrix for $250-350M to thwart rogue AI agents; how self-taught programmer Boris Cherny built Claude Code into a megahit propelling Anthropic to $30B ARR; SK Hynix and memory chip makers as undervalued AI plays trading at under 5x earnings vs Nvidia's 22x; OpenAI's Stargate data center leaders depart together to start a new company; PitchBook on agentic AI's $24B VC wave and the shift from per-seat to outcome-based pricing; and Exponential View on how Claude Mythos permanently fuses AI capability with danger and why critical infrastructure is systemically mispriced.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Cisco in talks to acquire Astrix AI security startup for $250-350M; Claude Code driving Anthropic to $30B ARR and the self-taught programmer behind it; SK Hynix and memory chips as undervalued AI plays; OpenAI Stargate leadership exodus; PitchBook on agentic AI's $24B VC wave and outcome-based pricing; and Exponential View on Claude Mythos fusing AI capability with danger and the mispricing of critical infrastructure cyber risk.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 10, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI Stargate leadership exits as three senior executives depart to start a new company; SpaceX-xAI loses nearly $5 billion last year from AI spending while planning the largest US IPO ever; OpenAI forecasts advertising revenue hitting $102 billion by 2030 — 36% of projected total revenue; Anthropic's Mythos AI model enables exploitation of cybersecurity vulnerabilities in minutes while defenders take days to patch; Meta launches new Spark model and Muse model family focused on personal superintelligence; former a16z GP Anjney Midha raises $1.3 billion for AMP, a new 'grid for AI' venture firm; Chapter's $3 billion AI Medicare broker backed by two vice presidents (Gore and Vance) triples revenue to $100M with just 33 employees; Western EV makers unlikely to benefit from high gas prices while Chinese BYD and Geely surge; Gary Marcus questions whether Anthropic's Mythos announcement was overblown; NFX's Omri Drory on why AI health and longevity is the last great abundance frontier; and the one-person AI company trend with lessons from MEDVi.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI Stargate leadership exits; SpaceX-xAI $5B loss from AI spending ahead of mega-IPO; OpenAI forecasts $102B in ad revenue by 2030; Anthropic's Mythos AI exploiting cybersecurity flaws faster than defenders can patch; Meta launches Spark/Muse AI models; former a16z GP Anjney Midha raises $1.3B for AMP; Chapter's $3B AI Medicare broker backed by two VPs; Western EV makers unable to capitalize on high gas prices; Gary Marcus on Mythos hype; NFX on AI health as the last abundance frontier; and the one-person AI company trend.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - April 9, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic surpasses $30B ARR overtaking OpenAI in annualized revenue; Nebius in exclusive talks to acquire AI21 Labs; Polygon Labs raising $100M for stablecoin payments platform; Amazon's tough pricing stance causing major brands to pull products; Claude Code GitHub public commits surging 25x in six months; GitButler raises $17M Series A from a16z for AI-powered git client; Intel joins Elon Musk's Terafab $25B solar compute initiative; Anthropic's Claude Mythos zero-day vulnerability hunter showing AI as the best cybersecurity researcher in the world; and The AI Agenda newsletter on software engineers' concerns about AI job displacement.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic surpasses $30B ARR overtaking OpenAI; Nebius in talks to acquire AI21 Labs; Polygon Labs raising $100M for stablecoin payments; Amazon's tough pricing stance causing brand pullouts; Claude Code GitHub commits surging 25x; GitButler $17M Series A from a16z; Intel joins Elon Musk's Terafab; Anthropic's Claude Mythos zero-day vulnerability hunter; and AI job displacement concerns from The AI Agenda.
